creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Stop Writing Tone Instructions. Layer Them. - Isadora Martin-Dye, Isadora & Co
videos.summary: A four-layer architecture for maintaining consistent brand voice in LLM applications: immutable identity, situational mode, example-anchored voice, and deterministic post-generation veto. The approach addresses the fragility of single-instruction prompts at scale by decomposing voice consistency into specialized layers, each handling different aspects of tone preservation.

videos.title: Turn 10,994 Notes Into Memory - Paul Iusztin, Decoding AI & Louis-François Bouchard, Towards AI
videos.summary: Paul Iusztin and Louis-François Bouchard present an AI Research OS system that converts 10,000+ Obsidian notes, documents, and repositories into structured context for AI agents. The system uses a three-layer architecture of raw content, indexing, and a Wiki layer to enable efficient deep research and retrieval without requiring vector or graph databases.

videos.title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
videos.summary: OpenGov's Gabe De Mesa describes how to build and scale AI agents in production across thousands of government workflows. The talk covers agent architecture patterns including core loops, feedback mechanisms, observability, and tooling approaches used in their OG Assist product.

videos.title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
videos.summary: Victor Savkin argues that current coding agents are severely limited by their inability to maintain context across interactions and see the full codebase, comparing them to a brilliant engineer with amnesia. He advocates for architectural improvements to enable greater agent autonomy and effectiveness in software development.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
videos.summary: Cole Medin analyzes Google's 50-page playbook on agentic engineering, arguing that AI model selection accounts for only 10% of results while the surrounding harness (context, tools, guardrails, verification) determines 90%. The video surveys the spectrum from vibe coding to agentic engineering and highlights context engineering and orchestration skills as critical for 2026.

videos.title: My AI Memory Now Follows Me Across Every Tool!
videos.summary: Walrus Memory is a memory layer that persists context outside individual AI agents and enables context sharing across different providers. It encrypts memories before storage and gives users control over which agents can access them. Setup requires only a single prompt.

videos.title: The New Kimi K2.7 Model is VERY Impressive
videos.summary: Kimi K2.7 Code is an open-source, coding-focused agentic model developed by Moonshot AI. Cole Medin reviews it as impressive for its price and size.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: Cole Medin describes how top AI engineers use automated loops to repeatedly prompt and iterate with agents rather than manual prompting. The approach involves an orchestrator agent spawning parallel worker agents with observability and database logging, reducing manual intervention but incurring higher token costs and potential hallucination compounding.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: OpenClaw Creator's new secret project...
videos.summary: AI Jason presents a secret project related to OpenClaw Creator. The video highlights Superdesign and references a GitHub repository for setting up Crabbox and codebase harness tools. A skill setup guide is available through AI Builder Club.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: AI Jason explains Loop Engineer and provides setup instructions. The video includes a GitHub repository with a codebase harness and knowledge template for Loop Engineer, plus a workshop in the AI Builder Club community.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: The video explains how to build proactive AI agents and self-improving company systems through autonomous feedback loops. It covers proactive AI loop construction, autonomous ads optimization and SEO use cases, and tools like memory and Loopany that enable agentic behavior.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses emerging autonomous coding capabilities via the /goal feature and related tools. The video covers setup, prompting strategies, and longer-horizon task execution through /Mission exploration, positioning these as steps toward real autonomous coders.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: GPT 5.6 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: OpenAI announces GPT 5.6, which faces government regulation as the AI industry matures. The video compares GPT 5.6 with Anthropic's paused models Mythos 5 and Fable 5, noting both face similar regulatory constraints. OpenAI has consolidated its model lineup to three offerings: Sol, Terra, and Luna.

videos.title: It's about time we learn Transformers..
videos.summary: A video tutorial explaining the fundamental mechanics of generative pretrained transformers, the architecture underlying major models like GPT, Gemini, and Opus. It covers self-attention, multi-head attention, positional embeddings, feed-forward networks, layer normalization, and residual connections.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code presents a 10-minute explanation of Andrej Karpathy's bigram language model, covering tokenization, embeddings, loss functions, and optimization. The video serves as a foundational introduction to language model mechanics before progressing to attention and GPT.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series including Nano, Super, and Ultra models. The architecture combines hybrid mamba transformer, MTP, and Latent MoE techniques.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: System Design for Beginners (Full Guide)
videos.summary: AWS Developers presents a structured 7-step framework for system design interviews that prioritizes problem understanding over technology selection. The approach covers requirements gathering, traffic estimation, high-level component design, API contracts, tech stack selection, and implementation constraints using a URL shortener as example.

videos.title: Your AI Agent Needs a Tool
videos.summary: Tool calling allows AI agents to request external functions to access real-time data and take actions, moving beyond stateless chatbot behavior. The technique involves model-generated tool requests, framework execution, and repeated agentic loops. Proper system prompts and tool schemas determine when agents invoke appropriate tools.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS provides AI coding agents with up-to-date documentation, best practices, and tested procedures for AWS services. It gives agents access to proper tools and guardrails to improve success rates and reduce errors when working with AWS infrastructure.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS Developers walkthrough on running Claude Code through Amazon Bedrock, enabling on-premise data residency and compliance. Covers environment setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, Bedrock Mantle endpoint, team cost tracking, and guardrails for content governance.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech explores whether Google engineers actually use informal code generation methods, comparing OpenAI Codex and Claude Code capabilities, and examining Google's approach to code generation across 2024 through 2026.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: A YouTube video by Alberta Tech discusses Malus, a company or product whose actions are framed as disruptive to open source development. The video covers how Malus works, its implications for open source maintainers, and whether the company's approach is problematic for the ecosystem.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov, co-founder of Gong, argues that software moats depend on customer context, proprietary data, and operational complexity rather than code alone. He contends that fears of AI disrupting SaaS are exaggerated and that the best AI products build raving fans through exceptional product experience.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that the robotics industry's focus on humanoid forms may be myopic and that future robotics adoption will depend on designing for human interaction and delight rather than utility alone. She predicts a ChatGPT-like moment for robotics as billions of robots enter society.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder and co-CEO of Pigment, discusses how an AI performance management platform displaced larger incumbents by building a comprehensive enterprise solution from the start rather than a point product. She covers why even innovative companies like Google rely on spreadsheets, the trust-building strategy that won major customers, and her hiring philosophy as a core competitive advantage.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, discusses how to distinguish real product-market fit from false signals. He argues that rabid fans do not prove PMF, that real PMF manifests as customer pull rather than push, and that founders should skip formal interviews in favor of embedded customer relationships to accurately assess product viability.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: AI Could Delete EVERYTHING! Businesses Ignored This! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses overlooked security threats posed by agentic AI, highlighting prompt injection, data deletion risks, and unauthorized actions as reasons businesses remain hesitant to deploy such systems despite their potential.

videos.title: AI Podcast: Anthropic's Latest & Lisbon's Electric Vibe #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof announces "This Is Tech Year" podcast episode featuring discussion of Anthropic's latest work. The episode includes guest Joao and references the Santos festival in Lisbon.

videos.title: Small Teams vs. Big Teams: Productivity & Meeting Madness! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof argues that small agile teams are more productive than large teams, which become bogged down in meetings and inefficiency. The piece contrasts team structure and organizational effectiveness.

videos.title: AI Memory Boom: Micron & SK Hynix Surge! Huge Growth Ahead! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses rising demand for high bandwidth memory driven by AI workloads. The video predicts growth opportunities for memory manufacturers Micron and SK Hynix as they address AI hardware needs.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
